prompt:
classic-laddie's system build is broken on current cosmo main — nixos-rebuild fails building the desktop theming stack. This blocks all deploys (including freshly-merged git-hosting and polkit changes waiting to activate), so it needs a minimal, fast fix.

ROOT CAUSE (from the failed rebuild on classic-laddie, 2026-07-12): building /nix/store/...-python3.14-catppuccin-2.5.0.drv fails at pythonImportsCheck with:

  File .../site-packages/catppuccin/__init__.py line 96: _register_styles()
  File .../catppuccin/extras/matplotlib.py line 87: catppuccin_stylesheets = mpl.style.core.read_style_directory(
  AttributeError: module 'matplotlib.style' has no attribute 'core'

i.e. catppuccin 2.5.0's matplotlib extra uses matplotlib.style.core, removed in the matplotlib version now in cosmo's nixpkgs pin (python 3.14 toolchain). The consumer chain is catppuccin-gtk-1.0.3 → python3 env with catppuccin → home-manager desktop theming (grep home/ and modules/common/desktop.nix for catppuccin to find where it enters).

FIX — pick the least-invasive that actually works, in this order of preference:
1. Check whether nixpkgs unstable already carries a fix (search nixpkgs master/unstable history and issues for 'catppuccin matplotlib style.core' — the breakage is recent and likely patched upstream). If yes, bump ONLY the nixpkgs input: nix flake update nixpkgs (or nix flake lock --update-input nixpkgs), and confirm the fix rev is included in the new pin.
2. Otherwise add a narrowly-scoped overlay in cosmo that unbreaks the build without changing behavior we use — e.g. override python3Packages.catppuccin to drop the matplotlib optional dependency (so extras registration never runs) or patch extras/matplotlib.py for the new matplotlib API. The desktop uses catppuccin for GTK theming, not matplotlib styles, so dropping the extra is behavior-neutral; justify in the PR.
3. Do NOT remove or swap the desktop theme itself — cosmetic churn the owner didn't ask for.

VERIFY by actually building, not dry-running (this machine has most of the closure cached): nix build .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel --no-link — must succeed. Also nix flake check. CI only dry-runs, so your local build is the real gate; say explicitly in the PR body that the toplevel built to completion locally.

PR body: root-cause summary (the traceback above), which fix path you took and why, and note this unblocks the pending first activation of the auto-rebuild loop (deploy note: someone still runs 'sudo systemctl start cosmo-rebuild' once after this merges).
